name: awesome-mobile-security
summary: > A practitioner-built reference for Android and iOS security — covering the full stack from app analysis and reverse engineering through kernel internals, malware research, and advanced platform exploitation. No fluff, no marketing copy. Built for security engineers who need to find the right tool or resource fast.

documentation.readme: > A practitioner-built reference for Android and iOS security — covering the full stack from app analysis and reverse engineering through kernel internals, malware research, and advanced platform exploitation. No fluff, no marketing copy. Built for security engineers who need to find the right tool or resource fast.
usage_sections: ZygiskFrida — Zygisk-based injection avoids frida-server process detection
Frida gadget mode — Embed frida-gadget.so directly in the app instead of running a server; harder to detect than an external frida-server process
Custom Frida builds — Rename Frida internals and recompile to evade string-based detection; community guides and forks available
objection's --startup-command — Inject bypass scripts at process initialization before detection logic runs
Shadow — Lightweight general-purpose jailbreak detection bypass. Low overhead, broad compatibility.

arguments.name: -aligned
arguments.description: insecure auth, data storage, network, IPC, code quality issues | `[Beginner–Intermediate]` |

arguments.name: -Analysis
arguments.description: **"A Systematic Analysis of Commercial Android Packers"** — ACM CCS 2022. Evaluates 12 commercial Android packer services, characterizing their protection mechanisms, detection eva

arguments.name: -Click
arguments.description: The BlastDoor Sandbox"** — Multiple speakers on the iMessage attack surface and the BlastDoor sandbox introduced in iOS 14 as a mitigation. Black Hat USA 2021. [Advanced]

arguments.name: -dive
arguments.description: what entitlements unlock, how the entitlement hierarchy is structured, and exploitation via entitlement abuse. OBTS v5.0. [Advanced]

arguments.name: -focused
arguments.description: iOS security architecture, vulnerability classes, and attack methodology. iOS 6-era (some chapters dated) but security architecture coverage remains foundational reading. `[Advance

arguments.name: -The-Wild
arguments.description: Excavating a Kernel Exploit"** — Seth Jenkins (Google). Analysis of kernel crash logs and artifacts from real-world Android exploit samples found in the wild. How to reconstruct ex

arguments.description: Force the action even if a safety check would block it.

chain_examples.note: Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with MobSF/Mobile-Security-Framework-MobSF (score=11); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with yeyintminthuhtut/Awesome-Red-Teaming (score=10); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with yeahqh/cloud-native-security-book (score=10); pair in the chain planner.
Phase 3: same attack_surface=['web'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.
Phase 4: same attack_surface=['web'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.
Phase 5: same attack_surface=['web'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

documentation.how_to_use: Clone or install `awesome-mobile-security` from its upstream repository, then run `awesome-mobile-security --help` (or the README entrypoint) to confirm flags. > A practitioner-built reference for Android and iOS security — covering the full stack from app analysis and reverse engineering through kernel internals, malware research, and advanced platform exploitation. No fluff, no marketing copy. Built for security engineers who need to find the right tool or resource fast.

trust.warning: Attribution/index entry only. Audit provenance, code, releases, and licence before use. Operates against the operator's own authorized scope only.

use_cases: When you need Curated mobile-security resource list, use awesome-mobile-security because > A practitioner-built reference for Android and iOS security — covering the full stack from app analysis and reverse engineering through kernel internals, malw.
When chaining Curated mobile-security resource list tools, pair awesome-mobile-security with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `awesome-mobile-security --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
